infobox.value: ([Northern Thai](/wiki/Northern_Thai_language "Northern Thai language"): ᨾᩯ᩵ᩈᩬᨯ; [Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): แม่สอด, pronounced [[mɛ̂ː sɔ̀ːt]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai"); [Burmese](/wiki/Burmese_language "Burmese language"): မဲဆောက်, [[mɛ́ sʰaʊʔ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Burmese "Help:IPA/Burmese"); [Shan](/wiki/Shan_language "Shan language"): မႄႈသွတ်ႇ, [[mɛ sʰɔt̚]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Shan_and_Tai_Lue "Help:IPA/Shan and Tai Lue")) is a city in western [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") that shares a border with [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar") to the west. It is notable as a trade hub and for its substantial population of Burmese migrants and refugees. The city is part of [Tak Province](/wiki/Tak_Province "Tak Province"), 87 km from the city of Tak and 492 km from Bangkok. It is home to the district headquarters of [Mae Sot District](/wiki/Mae_Sot_District "Mae Sot District"), and is the main gateway between Thailand and Burma. As a result, it has gained notoriety for its trade in gems and teak, as well as black market services such as [human trafficking](/wiki/Human_trafficking "Human trafficking") and illicit drugs.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/15/Thai-Myanmar_friendship_bridge.jpg/250px-Thai-Myanmar_friendship_bridge.jpg)](/wiki/File:Thai-Myanmar_friendship_bridge.jpg)

[Thai-Myanmar Friendship Bridge](/wiki/Thai-Myanmar_Friendship_Bridge "Thai-Myanmar Friendship Bridge") in [Mae Sot district](/wiki/Mae_Sot_district "Mae Sot district")

**Mae Sot** ([Northern Thai](/wiki/Northern_Thai_language "Northern Thai language"): ᨾᩯ᩵ᩈᩬᨯ; [Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): แม่สอด, pronounced [[mɛ̂ː sɔ̀ːt]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai"); [Burmese](/wiki/Burmese_language "Burmese language"): မဲဆောက်, [[mɛ́ sʰaʊʔ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Burmese "Help:IPA/Burmese"); [Shan](/wiki/Shan_language "Shan language"): မႄႈသွတ်ႇ, [[mɛ sʰɔt̚]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Shan_and_Tai_Lue "Help:IPA/Shan and Tai Lue")) is a city in western [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") that shares a border with [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar") to the west. It is notable as a trade hub and for its substantial population of Burmese migrants and refugees. The city is part of [Tak Province](/wiki/Tak_Province "Tak Province"), 87 km from the city of Tak and 492 km from Bangkok. It is home to the district headquarters of [Mae Sot District](/wiki/Mae_Sot_District "Mae Sot District"), and is the main gateway between Thailand and Burma. As a result, it has gained notoriety for its trade in gems and teak, as well as black market services such as [human trafficking](/wiki/Human_trafficking "Human trafficking") and illicit drugs.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Sot&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In 1937 Mae Sot was a local administration, administered by a headman, usually called village headman or village chief (ผู้ใหญ่บ้าน *phu yai ban*).[[2]](#cite_note-2) Its population at the time was approximately 12,000 people. On 30 September 1939 Mae Sot was established as a municipalitity[[3]](#cite_note-3) and governed 27 villages. It was upgraded to a city municipality in 2010.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 1997, the [Thai–Myanmar Friendship Bridge](/wiki/Thai%E2%80%93Myanmar_Friendship_Bridge "Thai–Myanmar Friendship Bridge") opened across the [Moei River](/wiki/Moei_River "Moei River") connecting Mae Sot with [Myawaddy](/wiki/Myawaddy "Myawaddy").[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) An additional border crossing, the second Thai-Myanmar Friendship Bridge, was opened In 2019 to the north west of Mae Sot to handle goods traffic.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In recent decades, the city has become home to thousands of [Burmese](/wiki/List_of_ethnic_groups_in_Myanmar "List of ethnic groups in Myanmar") migrants.[[7]](#cite_note-BBC2007-7) Industries in Mae Sot have grown, especially in the areas related to international trade and manufacturing in textiles, garments, gemstones, jade and teak.[[8]](#cite_note-BBC2007B-8) By 2004, the numbers of Burmese migrants in Mae Sot had reached between 70,000 and 100,000.[[9]](#cite_note-Arnold-9)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Sot&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

The neighboring districts of Mae Sot include Mae Pa [tambon administrative organization](/wiki/Tambon_administrative_organization "Tambon administrative organization") (TAO) in the north, Mae Tao (TAO) to the south, Mae Pa and Phra That Pha Daeng (TAO) to the east, and the Tha Sai Luat Sub-district Municipality to the west.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Sot&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Economy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/18/Rim_Moei.jpg/250px-Rim_Moei.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rim_Moei.jpg)

Rim Moei Market

Trade with Burma constitutes the largest portion of Mae Sot's economy. It has an established market for commodities such as wholesale gems and teak. Most of the town's service industries are supported by Burmese migrants who work in [sweat-shops](/wiki/Sweat-shops "Sweat-shops") and factories throughout the region. The town also suffers from a black market in illegal smuggling, human trafficking, and narcotics. The Thai-Myanmar Friendship Bridge is the primary gateway for trade with Burma. The border region, several kilometres west from central Mae Sot, includes the Rim Moei Market that deals in imported goods and woodwork.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Gen [Prayut Chan-o-cha](/wiki/Prayut_Chan-o-cha "Prayut Chan-o-cha"), the junta leader and prime minister, in May 2015 invoked his authority under Section 44 of the Interim Charter to declare the area of Wang Takhian in Tha Sai Luat subdistrict of Mae Sot a [special economic zone](/wiki/Special_economic_zone "Special economic zone") (SEZ). The current plan is to transform nearly 2,200 [rai](/wiki/Rai_(area) "Rai (area)") of land of Tha Sai Luat into an SEZ. The government has offered to compensate displaced villagers at 7,000-12,000 baht per rai of land and resettle villagers in another district of Tak. As of July 2016[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mae_Sot&action=edit) the current market price of a rai of land in Mae Sot is almost one million baht, leading villagers to reject the offer.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Mae Sot is located on the [East-West Economic Corridor](/wiki/East%E2%80%93West_Economic_Corridor "East–West Economic Corridor"), which connects [Burma](/wiki/Burma "Burma"), [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"), [Laos](/wiki/Laos "Laos") and [Vietnam](/wiki/Vietnam "Vietnam").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Sot&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Climate")]

Mae Sot has a [tropical savanna climate](/wiki/Tropical_savanna_climate "Tropical savanna climate") ([Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") *Aw*). Winters are dry and very warm. Temperatures rise until April, which is very hot with the average daily maximum at 36.8 °C (98.2 °F). The [monsoon](/wiki/Monsoon "Monsoon") season runs from May to October, with heavy rain and somewhat cooler temperatures during the day, although nights remain warm.

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mae_Sot&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Transportation")]

[Mae Sot Airport](/wiki/Mae_Sot_Airport "Mae Sot Airport") ([IATA](/wiki/International_Air_Transport_Association "International Air Transport Association"): MAQ, [ICAO](/wiki/International_Civil_Aviation_Organization "International Civil Aviation Organization"): VTPM) serves domestic flights.[[15]](#cite_note-15) In 2024, the airport was used to transport Myanmar military officials and families back to Myanmar. As of march 2025, Mae Sot airport only serves flights to and from Bangkok. [[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Mae Sot bus station is located adjacent to the airport.[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)
